title: Echo and Print
description: Output text and variables to the browser.

slides.title: Outputting Data
slides.content:
# Echo and Print

Both `echo` and `print` are used to output data to the browser.

*   `echo` is slightly faster and can take multiple parameters.
*   `print` returns a value (1) and can only take one argument.

In practice, `echo` is more commonly used.

slides.title: Using Echo
slides.content:
```php
<?php
echo "Hello";          // single string
echo "Hello", " World"; // multiple arguments (comma separated)
echo "Hello" . " World"; // concatenation with dot
echo 123;              // numbers are fine
?>
```
Note: `echo` is not a function (it's a language construct), so parentheses are optional.
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
echo "Hello";
echo "Hello", " World";
echo "Hello" . " World";
echo 123;
?>

slides.title: Using Print
slides.content:
```php
<?php
print "Hello";   // works
// print "Hello", " World"; // ERROR: print only takes one argument
$result = print "Hello"; // print returns 1, so $result = 1
?>
```

quizData.question:
What will the following code output?

```php
<?php
echo "First", "Second";
?>
```
quizData.explanation: `echo` with commas prints each argument sequentially, so they are concatenated without any separator.
